name: Tomatoes with Okra or Zucchini
recipeIngredient: An average of 12 pounds of tomatoes per canner load of 7 quarts
4 pounds of okra or zucchini per canner load of 7 quarts
An average of 7 pounds of tomatoes per canner load of 9 pints
2-1/2 pounds of okra or zucchini per canner load of 9 pints
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the tomatoes and okra or zucchini.
2. Dip the tomatoes in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds, then dip in cold water. Slip off the skins and remove the cores. Quarter the tomatoes.
3. Trim the stems from the okra and slice into 1-inch pieces, or leave whole if preferred.
4. Slice or cube the zucchini if using.
5. Bring the tomatoes to a boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
6. Add the okra or zucchini and boil gently for an additional 5 minutes.
7. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart of jars, if desired.
8. Fill the jars with the mixture, leaving 1-inch headspace.
9. Adjust the lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2, depending on the canning method used.
Variation: You may add four or five pearl onions, or two onion slices, to each jar.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Tomatoes with Okra or Zucchini in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
- Style of Pack: Hot
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 30 minutes for pints; 35 minutes for quarts
- Canner Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
Table 2. Recommended processing time for Tomatoes with Okra or Zucchini in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
- Style of Pack: Hot
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 30 minutes for pints; 35 minutes for quarts
- Canner Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
